title: Wholesome Japanese Beetroot Ramen
description: A cultured, creamy midnight-craving ramen featuring roasted beetroot, bhuna spice, and coconut milk, nut-free and perfect for Shikoku-inspired comfort.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and roast beetroot for 25 minutes until tender, then slice.
In a saucepan, heat coconut oil over medium heat, add bhuna spice paste and minced ginger, sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add vegetable broth and miso paste to the saucepan and stir until miso dissolves.
Reduce heat, pour in coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the roasted beetroot slices and season with sea salt, letting the soup simmer for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook ramen noodles in a separate pot according to package instructions, drain and set aside.
Divide the noodles into bowls, ladle the hot beetroot broth over them, and garnish with sliced scallions.
Serve immediately, optionally topping with a boiled egg half or a sprinkle of fresh cilantro.
